bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, and the opponent’s opening offer gives me the minimum value on every issue. I am targeting maximum utility with a strong opening counteroffer, while staying within the valid domain.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueF offer which is very low utility for me. I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD to avoid simply repeating my previous all-valueA offer.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04), so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly improved their offer to me from all valueF to issueA:valueE with the rest valueF, which is still very low for my utility, so I will continue holding firm on high-weight issues and concede only on the lowest-weight issueD while avoiding repeating my prior exact bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueF to issueA:valueE while keeping the rest at valueF, so I will continue conceding only on my lowest-weight issueD and hold firm on higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.08) and the opponent’s offers remain low for me, only moving slightly on issueA/issueB while keeping most issues at valueF. I will continue conceding mainly on issueD, my lowest-weight issue, while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC where my utility impact is much larger.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers remain far below my acceptable range despite small movement on issueA/issueB. Their history suggests they strongly prefer later values, so I am conceding only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest additional concession beyond my prior offers. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me but show small movement on issueA/B/C while consistently keeping issueD at valueF, so I will continue conceding on low-importance issueD and slightly on issueC while holding firm on my higher-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.856 because it is early in the negotiation, but I can continue conceding on lower-weight issues first. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and have only made small concessions, with no messages to justify a larger move, so I am holding firm on the high-importance issues A and B while moving further on issueC and keeping issueD fully conceded.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.824 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me, though they have started conceding slightly on issueA. I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB, while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and issueD to avoid simply repeating my last bid and to show movement.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.79 by holding firm on the two most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already-low issueD. At t=0.18 it is still early, so there is no need to match the opponent’s very low offers; their bids show only small, inconsistent concessions and remain poor for my utility.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, which is still strong for me while making a further concession on the lower-weight issues. At t=0.20 there is no need to make a large concession yet, and the opponent’s offers show only limited movement, mainly on issueA while holding issueB/issueC/issueD near valueF, so I will concede on issueC and issueD but hold firm on the more important issueA and issueB.

I targeted a still-high utility bid (about 0.79) because it is early in the negotiation at t=0.22, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has started conceding on issueA, even reaching my top value there, but they remain very tough on issueB, issueC, and issueD; I will hold firm on my high-weight issues A and B while offering concessions on lower-weight C and D.

At t=0.24 it is still early enough to target a strong utility around 0.78 while showing some movement on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, though they have occasionally conceded on issueA and most recently on issueB/issueD; I will hold firm on my most important issues A and B while conceding further on C/D to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.71 while making a meaningful early-mid negotiation concession. The opponent has mostly held issueC and issueD near valueF while recently moving issueA up to valueB/valueA and issueB to valueE, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA, concede slightly on issueB, and give ground on the lower-weight issues C and D.

At t=0.28 I am still targeting a strong utility above 0.70, since there is time left and the opponent has mostly offered low-utility bids to me despite some movement on issueA. Their best signals are concessions on issueA and occasional small movement on issueB/issueC/issueD, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA while offering meaningful concessions on lower-weight issues and avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73–0.76 because it is only t=0.30, so there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent has mainly held low-utility offers for me but has shown some movement on issueA and occasional concessions on issueB/issueC/issueD, so I will hold firm on the highest-weight issueA while offering concessions on lower-weight issues to explore acceptability without repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 while making a moderate concession appropriate for t=0.32. The opponent has mostly held firm near valueF but has shown willingness to move on issueA and recently issueD, so I will hold issueA at my highest-value option while conceding more on lower-weight issues and avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.75, since time is only at 0.34 and there is no need to concede down to the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent has started testing concessions on individual issues, especially issueA and issueB, but their offers still give me low utility, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while conceding more on lower-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, which is a modest concession from my prior high-utility bids while still protecting my most important issues, especially issueA and issueB. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure but not enough to make a large concession; the opponent has shown willingness to move on issueA and issueB separately, so I am combining firmness on issueA/B with concessions on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a utility in the low-to-mid 0.7s, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has shown only limited concessions and sometimes retreats after better offers. Their history suggests they may tolerate concessions on issueA and issueB occasionally, but they still push hard toward lower values overall, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and make concessions mainly on lower-weight issueC/issueD while avoiding an exact repeat of my prior bid.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding fully on the lower-weight issues C and D while holding my most important issueA at valueA and accepting the opponent’s recent movement to valueB on issueB. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA and issueB, but they still mostly demand valueF on C/D, so this bid uses their signaled preferences without over-conceding at t=0.40.

I targeted a utility around 0.65, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers while still protecting my highest-weight issueA from dropping too far. At t=0.42 we are in the mid-game, so I am matching the opponent’s latest signals on issueB, issueC, and issueD while countering their issueA:valueC with issueA:valueB. The opponent has gradually improved offers for issueA and issueB, so this bid uses that concession pattern without accepting their full position.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding meaningfully but still protecting my highest-weight issueA. The opponent’s offers have slowly improved for me, especially by moving issueA and issueB upward at times, but there are no messages to suggest reliable constraints; at t=0.44 I will avoid repeating my last bid and trade off more on issueB while holding issueA.
